crash.review.title: Wrapper crashed last time
crash.review.body: The wrapper crashed at {time}. Review the report below before deciding what to do.
crash.review.github_disabled: GitHub issue reporting not yet configured.
crash.review.preview_more: +{n} more crash reports — Dismiss marks all reviewed; see crashes/ folder for individual files.
log.preview.no_data_blocked: Read the controller or load a draft before starting preview.
log.preview.kept_as_draft: Temporary stick preview kept as the current draft.
log.preview.expired_reverted: Temporary stick preview expired and reverted.
log.config.loaded: Loaded saved PC config into the current draft.
log.config.no_selection_blocked: Select a saved PC config first.
log.checklist.unresolved: Symptom checklist kept the target unresolved.
firmware.checklist.modal_subtitle: Use symptom-first routing when you do not already know which maintenance target to open.
firmware.checklist.modal_helper: The checklist narrows the safest first target. It does not choose a firmware file or promise direct flashing.
firmware.checklist.q_receiver_scope: Is the problem specific to the receiver path, or does it also happen over cable?
firmware.checklist.q_after_firmware_or_r: Did the issue start after a firmware or repair step?
firmware.checklist.q_side_scope: Is the problem limited to one side or one stick path?
firmware.checklist.q_settings_or_recover: Did the issue start after changing settings, or after recovery/update work?
firmware.checklist.entry_helper: Answer a few questions and the app will choose the safest first maintenance lane.
firmware.checklist.helper_unresolved: The current route is still unresolved. The checklist can gather more structured symptom detail.
firmware.checklist.helper_side_unclear: If the side is still unclear, the checklist can safely narrow the next lane.
apply.axis_inversion.unavailable: {side} axis inversion not applied: settings service unavailable.
apply.back_paddle.unavailable: Back paddle binding not applied: settings service unavailable.
apply.binding.unavailable: Button binding not applied: settings service unavailable.
apply.binding.unknown_source: Apply failed: unknown source button '{source}'; binding not applied.
apply.binding.unknown_target: Apply failed: unknown target button '{target}'; binding not applied.
apply.busy: Controller operation in progress - try again in a moment
apply.deadzone.unavailable: Deadzone settings not applied: settings service unavailable.
apply.error.verify_failed: device did not commit the value (firmware rejected the write)
apply.lighting.unavailable: Lighting not applied: settings service unavailable.
apply.lighting.unknown_mode: Apply failed: unknown lighting mode '{mode}'; lighting not applied.
apply.lighting.unknown_zone: Apply failed: unknown lighting zone '{zone}'; lighting not applied.
apply.partial_failure_subtitle: {count} settings failed to apply. Review the list, then retry only the failed writes or dismiss this message.
apply.polling_rate.non_commit_8000: 8000 Hz requires controller firmware v1.18+; your controller kept its previous rate ({kept}).
apply.polling_rate.unavailable: Polling rate not applied: settings service unavailable.
apply.profile.device_confirm.body_intro: This profile includes device settings that affect the controller globally. "Apply profile + device settings" will write:
apply.profile.device_confirm.body_outro: Apply the profile only, or include its device settings?
apply.profile.device_confirm.title: Apply device settings?
apply.profile.partial: Partial: Applied profile '{name}' ({m} of {n} writes; {k} failed). See apply details.
apply.profile.save.no_name: Save failed: profile name is required.
apply.profile.save.no_snapshot: Save failed: no controller snapshot - click Refresh from Controller first.
log.read.official_summary: Read controller state with Official App UI summary fields.
log.read.official_summary_protocol_retai: Read controller state with Official App UI summary fields. Protocol-owned active config was retained.
log.read.official_summary_disconnected: Read official-app summary fields while direct controller presence was unavailable.
log.read.official_summary_disconnected_p: Read official-app summary fields while direct controller presence was unavailable. Protocol-owned active config remains trusted.
log.snapshot.refreshed_ok: Controller snapshot refreshed: OK: Controller read.
log.snapshot.refreshed_missing: Controller snapshot refreshed: Read failed for: {fields}
log.diagnostics.logs_cleared: Cleared diagnostics and device event logs.
apply.retry.partial: Partial: Retried failed settings ({m} of {n} writes; {k} failed).
apply.retry.success: OK: Retried {n} failed settings; all applied.
apply.sensitivity.unavailable: {side} sensitivity not applied: settings service unavailable.
apply.step_size.save_to_profile_failed: Couldn't save step size to profile '{name}': {reason}
apply.step_size.saved_to_profile: OK: Saved step size {value} into profile '{name}'.
apply.step_size.unavailable: Step size not applied: settings service unavailable.
apply.trigger.invalid_range: Apply failed: trigger min ({min}) > max ({max}); {side} trigger settings not applied.
apply.trigger.unavailable: {side} trigger settings not applied: settings service unavailable.
apply.trigger.unknown_mode: Apply failed: unknown trigger mode '{mode}'; {side} trigger settings not applied.
apply.vibration.unavailable: Vibration settings not applied: settings service unavailable.
apply.vibration.unknown_mode: Apply failed: unknown vibration mode '{mode}'; vibration settings not applied.
errors.no_name: Save failed: profile name is required.
errors.no_snapshot: Save failed: no controller snapshot - click Refresh from Controller first.
profile.skipped_count: {count} profiles could not be loaded - see log.
settings.app.subtitle: Settings stay intentionally small and predictable.
settings.app.title: Settings
settings.wrapper_v2.profiles.delete_conf: Delete profile {name}? This cannot be undone.
settings.wrapper_v2.profiles.name_input_: Name for new profile (e.g. Apex Tuned)
settings.wrapper_v2.profiles.subtitle: Save current settings as a named profile, then switch profiles per game.
settings.wrapper_v2.profiles.title: Wrapper Profiles
settings.wrapper_v2.subtitle: Direct controller settings via wrapper.
settings.wrapper_v2.title: Wrapper Settings (v2)
ui.3_anchor_curve_per_stick_defaults_to_: 3-anchor curve per stick. Defaults to linear.
ui.8k_requires_firmware_v1_18_lower_rate: 8K requires firmware v1.18+. Lower rates work on all firmware.
ui.activate_config_is_a_real_advanced_co: Activate Config is a real advanced controller action, but it is currently honest only while the official Controller Settings session is active.
ui.all_4_motors_trigger_mode_write_toget: All 4 motors + trigger mode write together.
ui.anchor_1_bottom_left_of_curve_anchor_: Anchor 1 = bottom-left of curve. Anchor 3 = top-right. Anchor 2 = midpoint shape control. Linear = (0,0), (50,50), (100,100).
ui.apply_writes_all_5_fields_together_ve: Apply writes all 5 fields together — changing one alone resets the others.
ui.apply_writes_all_populated_wrapper_se: Apply writes all populated wrapper settings sequentially. Delete is permanent.
ui.center_inactive_zone_near_rest_positi: Center = inactive zone near rest position. Outer = saturated zone near max travel. Apply writes all 4 atomically.
ui.choose_a_new_config_name_for_the_sele: Choose a new config name for the selected saved PC config.
ui.choose_a_target_or_start_the_symptom_: Choose a target or start the symptom checklist.
ui.configure_trigger_response_in_the_dra: This legacy screen edits a local draft only. Use the Triggers tab on the main Controller screen to apply trigger changes to the controller.
ui.connect_the_controller_then_retry_det: Connect the controller, then retry detection or open Diagnostics.
ui.connected_plus_live_packets_means_the:
Connected plus a recent read means the controller is talking to the app.
Read timings confirm refresh attempts.
Apply results are explicit: settings are verified by reading them back from the controller, and write-only bindings (back paddles) are reported as sent, not verified.
Support guidance is separate from protocol verification, so help copy never pretends a read or write succeeded.
ui.controller_detected_but_the_app_has_n: Controller detected, but the app has not read live settings yet.
ui.controller_summary_is_coming_from_the: Controller summary is coming from the official app and may lag behind a verified switch.
ui.current_values_are_local_defaults_unt: Current values are local defaults until you read the controller.
ui.default_keeps_the_stock_curve_safe_wi:
Default keeps the stock curve.
Safe widens the deadzone slightly.
Competitive reduces center deadzone while keeping outer protection.
Custom is unlocked as soon as you tweak any slider.
ui.defaults_are_light_on_always_on_mode_: Defaults are: Light On, Always On mode, full brightness, white RGB. Modes Breath/Fade/Flow animate; brightness/RGB still apply.
ui.direct_flashing_from_this_app_is_not_: Direct flashing from this app is not available yet. This screen routes you to the safest target and recovery steps.
ui.do_not_assume_one_update_target_fixes: Do not assume one update target fixes every issue. If the symptom changes after recovery, return to target selection before trying another lane.
ui.first_scope_only_lighting_mode_with_c: First scope only: Lighting Mode with clear transport truth. Off and Light On stay separate from this first write surface.
ui.if_the_user_does_not_know_the_target_: If the user does not know the target, route by symptom first. Do not begin with updater files or Update All language.
ui.keep_a_clear_boundary_between_the_cur: Keep a clear boundary between the current draft, saved PC configs, and onboard configs.
ui.keep_off_and_light_on_separate_keep_c: Keep Off and Light On separate. Keep color, brightness, zones, and bulk actions out of scope for now.
ui.live_meters_still_work_before_a_contr: Live meters still work before a controller read, but settings are local defaults until then.
ui.min_must_be_max_short_quick_activatio: Min must be <= Max. Short = quick activation, Long = full pull required.
ui.name_for_new_profile_e_g_apex_tuned_f: Name for new profile (e.g. Apex Tuned)
ui.on_direction_reversed_useful_for_flig: On = direction reversed. Useful for flight games (Y-invert) or off-hand-dominant setups (X-invert).
ui.paste_the_path_to_a_saved_zd_config_j: Paste the path to a saved ZD config JSON file.
ui.pick_a_source_button_pick_a_target_ac: Pick a source button, pick a target action, click Apply. Repeat for each remap.
ui.pick_a_zone_configure_it_click_apply_: Pick a zone, configure it, click Apply. Repeat for each zone you want to change.
ui.protocol_verification_is_authoritativ: Protocol verification is authoritative here. The official app summary can lag by a few seconds after an external switch.
ui.re_run_the_transport_probe_right_now_: Re-run the transport probe right now. The result is advisory only until a click-time ready gate succeeds.
ui.remap_physical_controls_into_a_local_: Remap physical controls into a local draft. Device writes stay explicit and visible.
ui.save_current_settings_as_a_named_prof: Save current settings as a named profile, then switch profiles per game.
ui.these_official_mode_labels_are_still_: These official mode labels are still out of the first normal write path, but they are not all equally mature.
ui.this_screen_is_intentionally_narrower: This screen is intentionally narrower than Profiles: mode semantics are usable, but lighting verification is still one step weaker than profile-switch verification.
ui.this_screen_is_the_trust_anchor_it_sh: This screen is the trust anchor: it should make reads, writes, and live input impossible to misread.
ui.to_reset_a_binding_set_target_action_: To 'reset' a binding, set Target Action to match Source Button. To unmap entirely, restore defaults via the official ZD Gaming Zone app.
ui.tune_deadzones_safely_temporary_previ: This legacy screen edits a local draft only. Use the Sticks tab on the main Controller screen to apply deadzone changes to the controller.
ui.use_read_controller_before_treating_t: Use Read Controller before treating the current editors as the source of truth.
ui.verification_transport_reachability_a: Verification: transport reachability alone does not count as success.
ui.windows_support_on_this_controller_is: Windows support on this controller is a stack, not one flat settings object. Keep runtime config, pairing, and maintenance flows separate.
ui.write_support_for_button_remaps_is_st: This legacy screen edits a local draft only. Use the Buttons tab on the main Controller screen to apply remaps to the controller.
status.config.label: Config:
nav.title: Navigation
footer.save_as_modal.title: Save current snapshot as...
footer.save_as_modal.include_device_hint: Device settings are global. Leave unchecked to save only this profile's feel, layout, and lighting.
footer.delete_confirm.title: Confirm delete
footer.delete_confirm.body: Delete profile '{name}'? This cannot be undone.
home.connection.title: Connection
home.profile.title: Profile
config.rename_modal.title: Rename Config
config.rename_modal.body: Choose a new config name for the selected saved PC config.
config.import_modal.title: Import Config
config.import_modal.body: Paste the path to a saved ZD config JSON file.
home.recent.title: Recent Activity
home.cta.title: Next step
home.cta.helper: Run a quick health check, or read the controller's current state.
controller.title: Controller
controller.subtitle: Tabbed controller settings backed by the wrapper protocol.
controller.no_service: The settings service is unavailable; controller settings cannot be read or applied.
controller.polling_rate.label: USB Polling Rate
controller.polling_rate.helper: 8K requires firmware v1.18+. Lower rates work on all firmware.
controller.polling_rate.unread: Couldn't read polling rate — click Read to try again.
controller.vibration.title: Vibration
controller.vibration.helper: All 4 motors + trigger mode write together.
controller.vibration.apply_note: Apply writes all 5 fields together — changing one alone resets the others.
controller.triggers.title: Triggers
controller.triggers.note: Min must be <= Max. Short = quick activation, Long = full pull required.
controller.triggers.left.title: Left Trigger
controller.triggers.right.title: Right Trigger
controller.sticks.deadzones_note: Center = inactive zone near rest position. Outer = saturated zone near max travel. Apply writes all 4 atomically.
controller.sticks.plot_8point.readout_hi: Drag a point to read its exact value.
controller.sticks.drag_hint: Drag the points on each graph to shape the curve, or fine-tune with the fields below. Nothing is written to the controller until you click Apply.
controller.sticks.sensitivity_note: Anchor 1 = bottom-left of curve. Anchor 3 = top-right. Anchor 2 = midpoint shape control. Linear = (0,0), (50,50), (100,100).
controller.sticks.sensitivity_8point_not: 8-point curve. Drag a point on the graph, type an exact value, or drag a slider; neighboring anchors auto-adjust to keep each X/Y ≥ the previous (monotonic). Apply writes all 8 pairs atomically.
controller.sticks.step_size.label: Stick Step Size
controller.sticks.step_size.helper: Smaller value = more precise stick response. Range 1-255 (default 73).
controller.sticks.step_size.unread: Couldn't read step size — click Read to try again.
controller.sticks.step_size.save_to_prof: Applying a profile can overwrite this. Save it so it sticks.
controller.sticks.inversion_note: On = direction reversed. Useful for flight games (Y-invert) or off-hand-dominant setups (X-invert).
controller.buttons.title: Button Bindings
controller.buttons.helper: Pick a source button, pick a target action, click Apply. Repeat for each remap.
controller.buttons.note: To 'reset' a binding, set Target Action to match Source Button. To unmap entirely, restore defaults via the official ZD Gaming Zone app.
controller.back_paddles.title: Back Paddles
controller.back_paddles.subtitle: Map each back paddle to one controller-button press.
controller.back_paddles.compatibility_no: Back paddles use 1-step button bindings only: one controller-button target, no keyboard targets, and no user-tuned timing sequence. This mode is meant to behave like ordinary controller-button input in games. Richer macros are not supported here and may move to a separate client later.
controller.lighting.title: Lighting
controller.lighting.helper: Pick a zone, configure it, click Apply. Repeat for each zone you want to change.
controller.lighting.note: Defaults are: Light On, Always On mode, full brightness, white RGB. Modes Breath/Fade/Flow animate; brightness/RGB still apply.
controller.motion.title: Motion
controller.motion.no_data: No motion settings were read from the controller yet.
controller.motion.subtitle: Motion settings are preserved in profiles but remain read-only in this wrapper.
controller.motion.read_only_note: Motion is read-only. Profile apply preserves the stored value but intentionally skips motion writes.
controller.profiles.title: Wrapper Profiles
controller.profiles.subtitle: Save current settings as a named profile, then switch profiles per game.
preferences.title: Settings
preferences.subtitle: App-wide preferences stay separate from controller tuning.
diagnostics.health.body.disconnected: No supported controller is currently active.
diagnostics.health.body.stale: Controller state may be out of date until the next read.
diagnostics.health.body.healthy: Live packets are arriving and diagnostics polling is active.
diagnostics.health.body.waiting: Controller is connected, but no new XInput packets have arrived yet.
diagnostics.health.body.never_read: Run a controller read before trusting editor values.
diagnostics.verify_bindings_note: To verify button/stick bindings, use an external gamepad tester such as hardwaretester.com/gamepad.
diagnostics.raw_hid.title: Raw HID frame log
diagnostics.raw_hid.empty: No raw HID frames captured yet.
diagnostics.legacy.title: Legacy screens
diagnostics.legacy.warning: Legacy advanced screens are deprecated and hidden by default.
support.calibration.summary: Choose the symptom first so you run the right calibration flow for the right part of the controller.
support.calibration.bullet.stick: Stick drift or centering issues map to Stick Calibration.
support.calibration.bullet.trigger: Trigger actuation, trigger lock feel, or trigger travel issues map to Trigger Calibration / Trigger Lock.
support.calibration.bullet.motion: Motion drift maps to Motion Calibration.
support.calibration.bullet.combined: Some ZD lines combine trigger and stick calibration. Follow the model-specific flow instead of calibrating only one part.
developer.show_dev_panels.label: Show developer diagnostics
developer.show_dev_panels.helper: Adds advanced diagnostics surfaces to the Diagnostics screen. Off by default; not needed for normal use.
ui.diagnostics.firmware_target_split: Firmware updates stay split by target: controller / receiver / left stick / right stick.
about.tagline: A local wrapper for reading and applying supported ZD Ultimate Legend controller settings.
about.disclaimer: This app is local-device tooling. It reports unsupported or unverified paths explicitly instead of pretending writes succeeded.
about.zd_disclaimer: This software is not developed or endorsed by ZD Gaming. Use at your own risk, and any controller issue caused by using this tool is not covered under the official warranty.
first_run.title: Before you begin
first_run.intro: This is an unofficial, community-made tool for the ZD Ultimate Legend controller. Please read and accept the following before you continue.
first_run.risk.writes: It changes your controller's settings by writing to the device over USB HID.
first_run.risk.hardware: It has been tested on limited hardware and firmware. It may behave differently on other firmware versions or controller variants.
first_run.risk.reversible: Changes are reversible: use the vendor app's "Restore to default", or this app's Restore Points, to undo them.
first_run.risk.as_is: This software is provided "as is", without warranty of any kind, to the maximum extent permitted by law. You use it at your own risk.
about.license_modal.title: Licenses
about.license_modal.section_intro: Bundled third-party components and their licenses.
safe_import.title: Safe Import
safe_import.header: Safe Import previews every change before saving or applying a shared profile.
safe_import.trust_statement: This app does not support macros, turbo, rapid-fire, timed sequences, game hooks, or input injection.
safe_import.file_select.title: Import Profile
safe_import.file_select.prompt: Choose a profile JSON file to preview before importing. Nothing is saved or applied yet.
safe_import.file_select.empty_error: Enter the path to a profile JSON file.
safe_import.summary.title: Safety scan
safe_import.categories.title: Supported settings
safe_import.categories.device_hint: Device settings can affect the controller globally. Review before applying them.
safe_import.categories.automation_hint: This file contained unsupported automation fields. They were blocked and will not be saved or written to the controller.
safe_import.diff.title: Changes
safe_import.diff.motion_never_applied: Read-only - imported for completeness; this app never writes motion settings to the controller.
safe_import.apply.footer: Recommended: save as a new profile first. You can apply it to the controller after reviewing.
safe_import.apply.confirm_body: This will write the selected settings to your controller. A restore point will be created first.
safe_import.result.title: Import complete
safe_import.result.write_sent: Settings sent to controller (not verified by read-back).
safe_import.result.write_sent_unverifiab: Settings sent - {count} field(s) could not be read back.
safe_import.result.write_sent_verify_fai: Settings sent - read-back failed; not verified.
safe_import.error.unreadable: the file could not be read
safe_import.error.invalid_json: the file is not valid JSON
safe_import.error.too_deep: the file is nested too deeply
safe_import.error.not_object: the file is not a profile object
safe_import.error.schema: the profile uses an unsupported version
badge.no_automation.label: No Automation
badge.no_automation.tooltip: This profile contains no macros, turbo, rapid-fire, or timed sequences recognized by this app. This is not a tournament or anti-cheat certification.
badge.automation_blocked.label: Unsupported Automation Blocked
badge.automation_blocked.tooltip: This file contained unsupported automation fields. They were blocked and will not be saved or written to the controller.
badge.device_settings.label: Device Settings
badge.device_settings.tooltip: This profile changes device-level settings such as polling rate and joystick step-size. Review before applying.
badge.needs_review.label: Needs Review
badge.needs_review.tooltip: Some fields in this file were unknown or could not be classified, and were ignored.
about.security.title: Security
health_report.preface.title: Controller Health Check
health_report.preface.body: This guided check reads HID input reports in this Windows session. It estimates stick rest noise, range coverage, and trigger travel. It does not measure end-to-end input timing or claim a true USB bus polling rate. For best results, close heavy background apps and avoid touching the controller except when prompted.
health_report.preface.label.local_only: This report is generated and stored locally. Nothing is uploaded.
health_report.ready.heading: Ready when you are
health_report.step.rest.title: Step 1 of 3 - Stick and trigger rest
health_report.step.rest.instruction: Place the controller on a flat surface. Do not touch the sticks or triggers.
health_report.step.rotation.title: Step 2 of 3 - Stick rotation
health_report.step.rotation.instruction: Slowly rotate both sticks around the outer edge 2-3 times. Try to reach every direction.
health_report.step.trigger.title: Step 3 of 3 - Trigger pull and release
health_report.step.trigger.instruction: Slowly pull both triggers all the way down, hold for one second, then slowly release.
health_report.summary.title: Health check complete
health_report.summary.overall_status.pos: Possible issue observed - retest before changing settings
health_report.summary.tile.range.title: Stick range coverage
health_report.summary.tile.range.caption: Depends on how fully the stick was rotated.
health_report.summary.tile.trigger.title: Trigger travel
health_report.table.rest.caption: Suggested deadzone is a practical starting point from this sample.
health_report.summary.footer_caveat: Local HID report snapshot. Not a hardware timing test or USB analyzer.
health_report.cancelled.title: Health check cancelled
health_report.cancelled.body: The check was stopped before it finished. No report was generated.
readiness_check.init.title: Readiness Check (quick mode)
readiness_check.init.body: A 20-second pre-match ritual. Samples rest noise, stick range, and trigger travel back-to-back, then gives a green / yellow / red verdict. Use it to verify before tonight's queue; use the full Health Check for a detailed report.
readiness_check.running.title: Running readiness check
readiness_check.phase.rest: Rest - place controller on a flat surface, hands off
readiness_check.phase.range: Range - slowly rotate both sticks around the outer edge
readiness_check.phase.trigger: Trigger - pull both triggers all the way down, then release
readiness_check.done.title: Readiness check complete
readiness_check.done.footer: Quick verdict from a 20-second sample. For a full report with exportable findings, run the Health Check.
readiness_check.status.green: Green - today this controller is trustworthy.
readiness_check.status.yellow: Yellow - one minor issue observed; usable, but worth a closer look.
readiness_check.status.red: Red - a clear problem was observed; run the full Health Check for details.
readiness_check.obs.rest_noticeable: Rest noise is noticeable - consider a slightly higher deadzone.
readiness_check.obs.rest_high: Rest noise is high - stick may be drifting.
readiness_check.obs.range_limited: Stick range coverage is limited - try a fuller rotation.
readiness_check.obs.range_retest: Stick range coverage was uneven - retest before changing settings.
readiness_check.obs.trigger_limited: Trigger travel did not reach a clean min/max - try a fuller press.
readiness_check.obs.trigger_noisy: Trigger reading was noisy - retest before changing settings.
readiness_check.obs.trigger_stepped: Trigger movement looked stepped - usable but worth a closer look.
readiness_check.obs.cadence_some_gaps: Observed report cadence had some long gaps.
readiness_check.obs.insufficient_samples: Not enough samples to score this run - run again.
readiness_check.cancelled.title: Readiness check cancelled
readiness_check.cancelled.body: The check was stopped before it finished. No verdict was produced.
restore_points.title: Restore Points
restore_points.subtitle: Saved snapshots of the controller settings this app could read.
restore_points.list.empty_state: No restore points yet. Restore Points are created automatically before higher-risk controller changes, and you can save one manually after reading the controller. They capture app-supported settings only; they are not factory backups.
restore_points.list.unavailable: Restore Points service is unavailable in this build.
restore_points.list.col.name: Restore point
restore_points.list.skipped_footer: {n} restore point(s) could not be read.
restore_points.list.footer_caveat: Restore Points capture app-supported, app-readable settings. They are not factory backups. After restore, the app verifies what it can and reports any fields that failed, could not be read, or did not match.
restore_points.delete_confirm.title: Delete restore point?
restore_points.confirm.title: Restore this snapshot?
restore_points.confirm.body: Restore supported settings from this restore point? The app will write the captured settings it supports, then read the controller back and show what matched. This may change Device settings such as polling rate or step-size if they were captured in this restore point.
restore_points.confirm.preview_no_op: No changes detected — this restore is a no-op.
restore_points.confirm.preview_unreadabl: ({n} fields couldn't be read — they'll be restored but the change can't be previewed.)
restore_points.in_progress.title: Restoring...
restore_points.in_progress.body: Writing captured settings to the controller, then reading back to verify.
restore_points.result.before_restore_not: A before-restore safety snapshot was saved as {title}.
restore_points.result.before_restore_ski: Could not save a before-restore safety snapshot — the current controller state was not readable.
restore_points.manual.helper: Captures the controller settings this app can currently read.
restore_points.manual.unavailable: Restore Points service is unavailable in this build.
trust_ritual.title: Controller verified
trust_ritual.config_only: HID config only. No drivers, no virtual devices, no background service.
trust_ritual.verified_writes: Settings are verified by read-back where the controller exposes a read path; write-only fields are reported as sent.
device_vs_profile.title: Device vs Profile
device_vs_profile.subtitle: Compare what's actually on your controller right now against a saved wrapper profile, field by field. Read-only — this screen never writes to the device.
device_vs_profile.unavailable: Profile comparison is unavailable in this build.
device_vs_profile.no_profiles: No wrapper profiles saved yet. Save a profile first, then come back to compare it against your controller.
device_vs_profile.no_device: Controller not connected. Showing the selected profile's values; connect your controller and click Read to compare.
device_vs_profile.pick_profile: Pick a profile to compare against your controller.
device_vs_profile.load_failed: Could not load the selected profile: {reason}
device_vs_profile.no_apply_recorded: No profile apply recorded yet — apply a wrapper profile to start tracking drift.
device_vs_profile.caveat: Read-only view. Back-paddle bindings can't be read from the device; motion settings are read-only and not written by this app.
wear_ledger.title: Wear Ledger
wear_ledger.subtitle: A chronological log of what's happened on this controller — sessions, applied profiles, restore points, health reports, slider commits, and service notes you've written yourself.
wear_ledger.unavailable: Wear ledger is unavailable in this build.
wear_ledger.empty_state: No events recorded yet. Events appear here as you use the wrapper. You can also add your own service notes (for example, when you install new modules).
wear_ledger.log.heading: Event log
wear_ledger.sparkline.heading: Recent verdict trend
wear_ledger.sparkline.empty: No health reports yet — verdict trend will appear here once you run one.
wear_ledger.sparkline.legend: {n} of last {max} verdicts shown (oldest left, newest right).
wear_ledger.modal.title: Add service note
wear_ledger.modal.body: Record a maintenance event for your own reference (module swap, stabilizing pads installed, recalibration after a drop, etc.). Notes are stored locally and not shared.
wear_ledger.modal.hint: What did you do to the controller?
modules.title: Modules
modules.subtitle: Per-side stick-module passports. Track each module's characterization history so you can compare modules across swaps and watch wear over time.
modules.unavailable: Module passport is unavailable in this build.
modules.list.heading: Active modules
modules.list.empty_card_body: No module assigned to this side yet. Click "Assign module" to record one.
modules.detail.sparkline.heading: Recent verdict trend
modules.detail.sparkline.empty: No runs yet — verdict trend will appear here after the first characterization.
modules.detail.sparkline.legend: {n} of last {max} verdicts shown (oldest left, newest right).
modules.assign.body_assign: Record an identifier for the module currently installed on this side. The label is freeform — use whatever helps you keep modules straight (e.g. "STOCK_BASELINE_LEFT", "K_SILVER_2026_05").
modules.assign.body_swap: Swapping archives the current passport (including all characterization history) and starts a fresh one for the new module. The archive stays on disk under module_passport/archive/ for future reference.
modules.assign.notes_hint: Where it came from, when installed, any quirks to remember.
modules.notes.title: Edit notes for {module_id}
modules.notes.body: Add free-text observations about this module. Replaces the existing note.
modules.notes.hint: Performance, feel, install date, anything you want to remember about this module.
modules.wizard.preface.title: Characterize the {side} module
modules.wizard.preface.body: This is a 60-second test. You'll hold the stick still (15s), trace the outer edge (30s), then let it return to rest (15s). Make sure the controller is on a flat surface and you're using both hands.
modules.wizard.ready_rest.title: Step 1 of 3 — Rest (15s)
modules.wizard.ready_rest.body: Place the controller flat. Let the stick center itself naturally — don't touch it. When ready, click Begin step.
modules.wizard.ready_rotation.title: Step 2 of 3 — Rotation (30s)
modules.wizard.ready_rotation.body: When ready, trace the outer edge of the stick range. Steady circular motion, both directions if you have time. Click Begin step to start the 30-second window.
modules.wizard.ready_settle.title: Step 3 of 3 — Settle (15s)
modules.wizard.ready_settle.body: When ready, push the stick to one side and release it cleanly so it returns to center. Don't touch it after release. Click Begin step.
modules.wizard.running.title: Characterizing {side}
modules.wizard.complete.title: Characterization complete
modules.wizard.cancelled.title: Characterization cancelled
modules.wizard.cancelled.body: No fingerprint was saved. Click Run characterization to try again.
modules.confirm.swap_body: This archives the current passport ({module_id}) and its {runs} characterization run(s). The archive stays on disk; the active passport is replaced.
modules.confirm.swap_confirm_label: Type the new module ID to confirm
modules.archived.title: Archived {side} passports
modules.archived.empty: No archived passports for this side yet.
modules.archived.detail_history_empty: No characterization runs were recorded for this module.
modules.compare.title: Compare left + right modules
modules.compare.delta_unavailable: Both sides need at least one characterization run to compare.
modules.compare.delta_caveat: Heuristic hints only — these aren't wear claims, just side-by-side numbers worth a second look.
modules.export.row_body: Generate a shareable bundle of module passports, recent Health Reports, and a wear-ledger summary. Useful if you want concrete evidence of wear/tear to share with the controller maker.
modules.export.unavailable: Diagnostic bundle export is unavailable in this build.
modules.export.modal_body: Aggregate already-recorded wrapper data (module passports, recent Health Report exports, wear-ledger roll-up) into a single shareable file. All data stays on your machine — sharing is manual.
modules.export.claim_boundary_footer: The bundle includes a claim-boundary statement that describes what observed-HID measurements can and cannot prove. Report content is English-only for shareability.
modules.trends.title: Trend analysis — {side} ({module_id})
modules.trends.subtitle: Per-metric movement over time. Advisory only — these are linear fits across your recorded fingerprints, not a wear claim about your stick.
modules.trends.unavailable: Trend analysis is unavailable in this build.
modules.trends.sample_summary: {n} usable fingerprint(s) in the trend fit.
modules.trends.metric_row.projection: Projected to reach wear-observed threshold in ~{days} day(s) if the trend holds.
modules.trends.metric_row.no_projection: No actionable projection — slope too flat, confidence too low, or metric already in the wear-observed band.
modules.trends.attention_banner.title: Attention — trend movement worth a closer look
modules.trends.attention_banner.body: {n} metric(s) are trending toward the wear-observed threshold: {metrics}. Consider re-running characterization or swapping the module if the trend continues.
modules.trends.recharacterize_hint: Last characterization was over {days} days ago. Re-run to refresh the trend fit.
diagnostics.trust_anchor_intro: This screen is the trust anchor: it should make reads and writes impossible to misread.
diagnostics.actions.bundle_note: Bundle includes your controller's hardware ID (VID/PID).
diagnostics.stale_warning.headline: Controller state may have changed outside the app.
diagnostics.stale_warning.helper: Read the controller again before trusting summary fields or write context as current.
diagnostics.trust.body:
Connected plus a recent read means the controller is talking to the app.
Read timings confirm refresh attempts.
Apply results are explicit: readable settings are verified by reading the value back from the controller; write-only fields (back-paddle bindings) are reported as sent.
Support guidance is separate from protocol verification, so help copy never pretends a read or write succeeded.
log.diagnostics.bundle_unavailable: Diagnostic bundle service is not available.
diagnostics.live_verify.title: Live Verify
diagnostics.live_verify.intro: Press buttons and move the sticks to see exactly what Windows (XInput) reports - no external tester needed. Spin a stick at full deflection to measure its circularity.
diagnostics.live_verify.availability.una: XInput is unavailable on this system.
diagnostics.live_verify.availability.no_: No controller detected. Connect a controller to see live input.
diagnostics.live_verify.error_gated: Circularity error: {pct}% (keep spinning to finish the sweep)
diagnostics.live_verify.circularity_help: Circularity error is a preference, not a fault - around 8% is typical for a stock controller. A smaller deadzone lowers it; that is not inherently better.
diagnostics.live_verify.buttons.title: Live Buttons
diagnostics.live_verify.triggers.title: Live Triggers
diagnostics.live_verify.deadzone.title: Inline Deadzone Tuning
diagnostics.live_verify.deadzone.note: These sliders write the controller's real firmware deadzones (debounced and verified) - the circularity envelope above moves because the controller's output actually changes. A Restore Point is captured first, so you can revert.
Written, but read-back did not match.
Connect a controller to tune deadzones.
